classification.sub_class: bulking agent, anti-caking agent, texture modifier, emulsifier

natural_occurrence.description: Cellulose is the most abundant organic polymer on Earth, forming the structural framework of plant cell walls. When you eat fruits, vegetables, or whole grains, you're consuming cellulose as dietary fiber. Food-grade cellulose is purified from wood pulp or cotton.

manufacturing.description: Microcrystalline cellulose (E460i): Wood pulp or cotton is treated with dilute acid to remove amorphous (non-crystalline) regions, leaving highly crystalline microcrystals. These are then spray-dried to produce fine particles. Powdered cellulose (E460ii): Wood pulp is mechanically processed and purified to produce fine cellulose powder. Both forms are essentially purified, processed plant fiber.

other_uses.cosmetics: Bulking agent, absorbent, and viscosity controller in cosmetics.
other_uses.industrial: Paper production, textile fibers, construction materials, biofuels.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Tablet binder and disintegrant (most common pharmaceutical excipient), capsule filler.
other_uses.household: Paper products, cotton textiles, cellulose sponges.

eu.conditions: Widely permitted in most food categories; considered inert dietary fiber.

commonly_found_in: Low-calorie and reduced-fat foods (adds bulk without calories)
Dietary supplements and vitamin tablets (binder and filler)
Ice cream and frozen desserts (prevents ice crystal formation)
fun_facts: When you eat cellulose, you're literally eating processed wood pulp or cotton fiber - but it's completely safe and natural plant material!
Humans can't digest cellulose (we lack the enzyme cellulase), so it passes through as dietary fiber - it's calorie-free bulk
Cellulose is the most abundant organic compound on Earth - there are about 1.5 trillion tons of it
The shredded cheese in your refrigerator stays separated thanks to cellulose coating - otherwise it would clump into a solid mass
Microcrystalline cellulose is the world's most common pharmaceutical ingredient - most tablets contain it as a binder
